_comment: Config for scripts/readiness.py (see RELEASE_READINESS.md). Signals, available_models, weights and targets are read from here so the script stays byte-identical across projects. 'panels' starts empty: append one object per review panel (its participating models and NEW confirmed findings by severity) as panels are run, then re-run scripts/readiness.py.

panels.note: First panel. HIGH (consensus, 3 models): 7z backend broken against py7zr>=1.0 which removed SevenZipFile.read() (default suite blind, tests skip without py7zr). MEDIUM (singleton, reproduced): ZIP symlinks read as link-target bytes, not b'', violating the special-member-empty contract the tar backend honoured. LOW: _human_size emitted '1024.0P' past the petabyte unit.

MEDIUM (consensus, opus+sonnet): truncated gzip/xz leaked a raw EOFError/LZMAError from _SingleFileBackend._streamed_size past the exception hierarchy and crashed the CLI; now normalised to ArchiveReadError. MEDIUM (singleton haiku, reproduced; right-sized from HIGH): an over-NAME_MAX member name raised an uncaught OSError(ENAMETOOLONG) aborting extraction; now recorded in skipped_unsafe. Dismissed: haiku 'CRITICAL' single-file read() ignoring name (harmless single-member leniency; sonnet concurred below-threshold) and a cosmetic return-type NIT.

MEDIUM (singleton sonnet, reproduced): 7z backend never flagged symlink members (is_special hardcoded False) though py7zr exposes is_symlink -- they extracted as empty files, inflated count, skipped skipped_links, and made inspect() raise on escaping symlinks; now detected/skipped like zip/tar (no host-file disclosure since py7zr blocks escapes, hence MEDIUM not HIGH). LOW (opus NIT / haiku MEDIUM, converged): missing-member reads were inconsistent (zip/tar raw KeyError, 7z silent b'', single-file returned payload); normalised to uniform ArchiveReadError + documented, superseding the panel-2 leniency dismissal. NIT (opus): 7z solid-block compressed_size=0 -- same class as the documented tar limitation, extended that LIMITATIONS entry.

Near-clean panel: sonnet and haiku found ZERO defects; opus found one LOW. LOW (singleton opus, reproduced): an encrypted-header 7z with a missing password was mislabeled with the generic 'corrupt or unsupported 7z archive' message (indistinguishable from a genuinely bad file) and surfaced at open time; now raises UnsupportedArchiveError naming the password cause, with the open() docstring amended. Wrong-password keeps the generic path (garbage-parse, indistinguishable from corruption). Read-only-unverified rar observations were noted but not actionable without the rar binary. First below-tau (clean) panel: weighted yield 1.0.

Gate-deciding panel broke it open (yield non-monotonic 1.0 -> 6.4): proof that Panel 4's near-clean alone was not convergence, and why two consecutive clean panels are required. MEDIUM (opus, reproduced): 7z member with an interior '..' (docs/../report.txt) extracted as an EMPTY file -- silent data loss; py7zr writes the normalised path while read() reconstructed the raw name; fixed via os.path.normpath. LOW x2 (sonnet, reproduced): the ENAMETOOLONG class Panel 2 fixed for the non-flat path still crashed the flat-mode (_unique_basename) and recursive (_unique_dir) siblings -- the exact 'flat-mode crash that hid behind the non-flat one'; now recorded in skipped_unsafe / skipped_nested. NIT x2 (haiku, reproduced): ZIP and 7z open_stream returned a stream not None for directory members (interface-contract violation, no functional impact); fixed for symmetry. Clean streak RESET to 0.

haiku clean. MEDIUM (opus, reproduced): the Panel-5 escape guard 'norm.startswith("..")' over-matched legitimate basenames beginning with two dots (..notes.txt, ...txt, ..foo), reading them as b'' -- silent data loss, a NEW instance of the same class the guard fixed (a fix that spawned a sibling bug). Tightened to a true leading-'..' component check. LOW (sonnet, reproduced): overwrite=False misclassified a same-archive normalised duplicate as skipped_existing instead of skipped_collisions due to check order; reordered (written_targets before overwrite/exists). Data always correct. Clean streak still 0 -- a fix-induced MEDIUM and an audit-bucket LOW. AFTER this panel the 7z read path was ROOT-CAUSE refactored (commit d3cd07a) from extract-to-temp+path-reconstruction to an in-memory BytesIOFactory read keyed by py7zr's own member name, eliminating the whole data-loss class.

First clean panel after the BytesIOFactory refactor; weighted yield 1.0 (below tau). opus attacked the refactored read path directly (factory key-mismatch surface, normalization-duplicate names, limit boundary, hostile names) and found ZERO -- evidence the mechanism change closed the recurring 7z seam. LOW (sonnet, reproduced): recursive 7z enforces max_member_bytes before the include/exclude filter (non-streaming members must be materialised to sniff for nesting); documented as an intentional cost-of-fix tradeoff. DISMISSED haiku 'HIGH' duplicate-member-name read: opus+sonnet independently judged it correct (by-name APIs cannot disambiguate duplicates; extraction records them in skipped_collisions, nothing lost); documented as a fundamental-ambiguity limitation. Both behaviours pinned with tests. Clean streak now 1 of 2.

CLEAN at full diversity -- all three models found ZERO defects across a broad assault deliberately steered OFF the now-closed 7z seam onto safety.py (traversal/realpath/drive-letter/UNC/control), detect.py thresholds, cap enforcement at N vs N+1 with partial-result cleanup, artifacts four-corner tests, models invariants, CLI exit codes, and the single-file backends (sonnet alone ran ~35 real-input repros). The Panel-7 BytesIOFactory refactor held. Only the long-standing rar directory-member asymmetry was noted again as READ-ONLY-UNVERIFIED (no rar binary) -- not a confirmed finding. Second consecutive full-diversity clean panel; this earned RELEASABLE on the PRE-consolidation tree.

CONFIRMATION panel after the hhhw8t consolidation + CI fix (those changed converged code, so the Panels 7-8 streak was stale). haiku and sonnet found ZERO; opus found one LOW regression from the consolidation: a non-archive named like an archive (text 'decoy.zip') inside a 7z escaped include/exclude because extension-based detection presumed it a container, it failed to open, and was extracted as a leaf -- the streaming backends content-sniff and filter it. Fixed: re-apply the leaf filter when an extension-classified container fails to open, unwinding the write/budget. Also fixed (separate, before this panel): CI was red since Panel 3 on the py7zr 0.20.8 matrix leg (FileInfo.is_symlink only exists in py7zr>=1.1) -> dep bounded py7zr>=1.1,<2, CI matrix [1.1.0,1.1.3], CI now green. NOTE: the decoy fix (commit 0ebc8f9) changed code AFTER this panel, so a Panel-10 confirmation on the post-fix tree is required to honestly re-establish the clean streak.

CONFIRMATION of the Panel-9 decoy fix. LOW (CONSENSUS opus+sonnet, reproduced): a sibling bug in the Panel-9 fix -- _unwrite_filtered_leaf released ctx.written_targets/file_count/total_bytes but NOT the flat_used basename reservation, so in flat=True a filtered-out decoy left a stale reservation and a later legit member was spuriously renamed 'name (1)' (cosmetic, no data loss). Fixed (e294e52): release flat_used.discard(target.name) when ctx.flat. opus+sonnet both verified the unwrite accounting is otherwise fully consistent with disk (caps, depth, duplicates, streaming). haiku AND sonnet reviewed the POST-fix HEAD e294e52 and found it CLEAN; opus reviewed the pre-fix f2daea3 and its only finding was the now-fixed flat_used leak. Third consecutive fix-induced sibling in the extension-detection feature (Panel 9 decoy, Panel 10 flat_used) -- but the accounting unwind is now comprehensively covered.

FINAL confirmation of the extension-detection feature -- which yielded a defect in THREE straight panels. haiku clean. opus LOW (reproduced): an archive-NAMED, oversized, filtered-out 7z member trips max_member_bytes before the filter and aborts the whole extraction (the extension sniff let archive-named members bypass the filter; the documented 'uniform caps' claim only held for non-archive names). sonnet NIT: empty parent dir left after a non-flat decoy unwrite (write-then-open asymmetry). RESOLUTION per the pre-agreed guardrail: REVERTED the extension-based detection to the pre-consolidation content-sniff (peek) approach (commit 0223eab), eliminating all four extension-feature defects (Panels 9-11) at once and restoring more-correct behaviour (a real archive under a non-archive name is now recursed). The revert restores code that was panel-clean for Panels 1-8; the documented cap-before-filter limitation is reinstated. NOTE: the revert changed code, so a Panel-12 confirmation on the reverted tree is needed to honestly close the streak -- though every remaining component has now been panel-reviewed clean (peek recursion in Panels 1-8; BytesIOFactory/dest-guards/DEL/py7zr-bound in Panels 9-11).

CLEAN confirmation on the reverted tree (a70af86). opus and haiku found ZERO; sonnet found ZERO above NIT, with one NIT: _split_ext('file.') returns ('file','.') while _extension strips the trailing dot, so two trailing-dot-named members collide to 'file.' / 'file (1).' in flat mode (cosmetic, no data loss, pathological trailing-dot filenames). DISMISSED as below the action threshold (fixing it would reset the panel for no real benefit). All three models verified the content-sniff recursion: a real archive under ANY name is recursed; a decoy (archive-named text) is a leaf and honours include/exclude; office docs stay leaves; the cap-before-filter limitation is name-agnostic and matches LIMITATIONS.md; mid-recursion accounting and partial-result cleanup are consistent; full cross-backend sweep clean. This is the first full-diversity clean panel on the exact shipping tree; the reverted recursion is the Panels 1-8 code and every other component was Panels 9-11 clean -- the long-tail trajectory across the confirmation effort decayed LOW(9) -> LOW(10) -> LOW+NIT(11) -> NIT-only(12).

CONFIRMATION of the Panel-12 trailing-dot NIT fix (cee47c7: _split_ext splits on basename.rstrip('.').rfind('.') so 'file.' has no extension and a flat-mode collision lands as 'file. (1)'). opus and sonnet both CLEAN (ZERO findings); each independently reproduced the multi-dot case ('a.b.c.' -> stem 'a.b', ext '.c.', collision 'a.b (1).c.') and confirmed it is lossless (stem+ext==basename), unique, and writable -- sonnet even built an archive containing BOTH 'a.b.c.' and 'a.b (1).c.' and verified all three payloads land distinctly. DISMISSED haiku 'HIGH': it read the docstring's 'matching detect._extension' as a promise of byte-identical extensions and flagged ext='.c.' as a contract breach -- but the real contract is losslessness of the rename, which holds; haiku's own proposed fix (split ext from the stripped name) DROPS the trailing dot ('a.b (1).c'), making stem+ext != basename -- a lossy regression, proven by direct repro. Residual: the comment's parenthetical example DID mislead a reviewer, so it was clarified and a multi-dot regression test added (test_flatten_collision_multidot_trailing_dot_name) -- comment+test only, no behaviour change, so the clean verdict stands on the unchanged _split_ext logic. Second consecutive full-diversity clean panel on the shipping tree (Panel 12 NIT-only, now fixed; Panel 13 clean) -- the long-tail trajectory completes LOW(9)->LOW(10)->LOW+NIT(11)->NIT(12)->CLEAN(13). 280 passed / 1 skipped, ruff+mypy clean.

CLEAN convergence confirmation on the shipping tree (9cb5784 -- the Panel-13 comment-clarification + multi-dot regression test). All THREE models found ZERO defects at every severity. Each verified the reworded _split_ext comment is now ACCURATE (only the split *index* matches detect._extension; trailing dots are deliberately kept inside ext so stem+ext==basename) and that the new test asserts the TRUE lossless behaviour. The Panel-13 false HIGH was explicitly refuted by all three. Sharpest stress yet: opus and sonnet both built an archive whose members include a literal collision-candidate name (a.b.c. x2 PLUS a real a.b (1).c.) and confirmed the generated suffix bumps past it (a.b (1) (1).c. / a.b (2).c.) with no data loss. Broad surface re-swept clean (safe_target traversal/DEL/UNC/drive-letter; detect thresholds N vs N+1; 7z BytesIOFactory fidelity + content-sniff recursion; caps N/N+1 + partial cleanup; is_os_artifact four corners; CLI exit codes; single-file gz/bz2/xz; cross-backend symmetry). Third consecutive full-diversity clean panel on the exact shipping tree (12 NIT-only -> 13 clean -> 14 clean). 280 passed / 1 skipped, ruff+mypy clean. Blind spot unchanged: rar CONTENT path untestable without the rar/unrar binary.
